schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; the design is Akien-ratified (signature gate, 2026-07-17 'I think we're done, yes. CC++').
intention: Make the node state-machine physics: the emit-chokepoint enforces the transition table over the state vocabulary (the -ME summons states; the PROVED / LEARNING rests), validates every workflow string against a known versioned node-class definition, and journals every transition and every workflow migration. The design lives in the MAP stone 'The node state-machine — states as summons'; this node is the work to turn that prose into enforcement (Law 4).
why: Today /sorted 'refuses illegal transitions' only as prose in the skill — an IOU. The vocabulary is ratified but not yet enforced; until the chokepoint enforces it, the refusal is policy, not physics. This node is where the vocabulary becomes the physics the whole ticket system runs on (state IS the pipeline instance). Cast self-hosting: the first node whose own `state` is written in the vocabulary it will enforce.
traces_to: Law 1 (state IS the pipeline instance — no re-derivation)
MAP 'The learning loop is the primitive'
state: code-seam@v1: THINKME -> [TICKETME] -> BUILDME -> PROVEME -> LEARNME -> PROVED   (cursor at TICKETME: the design (THINKME) is wrapped + ratified and decomposed into the children below; BUILDME waits on the emit-chokepoint, which is part of the base-class / CP-diagnostic spine step. So this node is cast and decomposing, waiting on the base class — a parent-infrastructure dependency — not buildable yet.)
gates: proof gate (tester) — IOU. The proof a hollow build could not pass: construct a node whose workflow attempts an illegal transition and show the chokepoint refuses it; migrate a workflow by string-edit and show the journal crossing + version-validation; a driver whose method-pointer is unproven is refused (Law 8). Durability gate (code-seam): tested AND committed.
falsifier: the vocabulary can't express a real node's lifecycle, OR a node arises needing a state/transition the vocabulary lacks, OR the -ME grammar rule mis-names a state (a summons with no -ME, or a node's-own-condition carrying one). Wrong-shape signal: if enforcing the transition table makes casting so rigid that real work routes around it, the table is wrong, not the work.
horizon: first real enforcement — when the emit-chokepoint (base-class spine step) lands and the first node's transitions are gated by physics rather than the /sorted skill's prose.
children: (pending) complete the transition table — all legal edges incl. back-edges by severity (PROVEME red -> BUILDME / TICKETME / THINKME; ask-Akien escalation for very-wrong) and the dispositions (DROPPED / SUPERSEDED / RETIRED) fully specified.
(pending) concept-piece workflow finalized (THINKME -> REVIEWME -> PROVED; learns by supersession, not LEARNME).
(pending) the emit-chokepoint that validates workflow strings against node_classes/ versioned definitions and journals transitions + migrations — belongs to the base-class / CP-diagnostic spine step.
(pending) ground_loop-as-driver-executor — confirm or refute the hypothesis when the ground loop is cast; and the method-registry that operational-driver's method-pointer resolves against.
waits_on: the emit-chokepoint (base-class / CP-diagnostic base spine step) — the vocabulary cannot be enforced before the chokepoint that enforces it exists. Design-complete now; buildable when the base class lands.
provenance: cast 2026-07-17 via a hand-run /sorted at the close of the state-vocabulary design session (post-compact). The design converged across the session: horizon-of-awareness -> LEARNING/sleep -> tickets-as-operational-drivers -> workflow-as-mutable-string. Ratified by Akien 'I think we're done, yes. CC++'. Self-hosting: the first node cast in its own vocabulary.
